question: What is the volume of the cylindrical section of the PZR region?

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the necessary valve testing procedures, and specifies that an in-situ DHRS heat removal test will be conducted on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is responsible for removing noncondensible gases from the pressurizer (PZR) during both normal and emergency operations, and for maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS, located inside the containment vessel (CNV), includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), integral PZR, and associated piping and controls, and operates primarily by natural circulation to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generators (SGs).

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It emphasizes compliance with specific codes for packaging, handling, and quality assurance, as well as maintaining a controlled environment during shipment to ensure vessel integrity. Key references to industry standards are also provided.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across various power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures. These parameters are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties and design margins considered to ensure efficient operation and safety. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties like SG tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow determination.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integral to the RPV.

edges.description: Section A defines the physical boundaries and configuration of the pressurizer region (PZR), which is the specific volume referenced in Section B as the PZR volume for system operations such as degasification and venting.
